content: Heart failure (HF) is a clinical syndrome characterized by typical symptoms (dyspnea, fatigue, fluid retention) and signs (elevated JVP, crackles, peripheral edema) due to structural or functional cardiac abnormality. Classification by ejection fraction: HFrEF (reduced EF ≤40%), HFmrEF (mildly reduced EF 41-49%), HFpEF (preserved EF ≥50%). ACC/AHA stages: Stage A (at risk, no structural disease), Stage B (structural disease, no symptoms), Stage C (structural disease with current/prior symptoms), Stage D (refractory HF requiring advanced therapies). NYHA functional class I-IV describes symptom severity. Pathophysiology involves neurohormonal activation (RAAS, sympathetic nervous system, natriuretic peptides), ventricular remodeling, and progressive dysfunction. Common etiologies: ischemic heart disease, hypertension, valvular disease, cardiomyopathies (dilated, hypertrophic, restrictive), arrhythmias, toxins (alcohol, chemotherapy).
